reasoning: The data from Box Office Mojo as of August 2025 shows that the current highest-grossing film of the year is "Ne Zha 2" with a whopping $2.26 billion. The next 7 films on the list, including "Zootopia 2" ($1.86 billion) and "Avatar: Fire and Ash" ($1.48 billion), are also solidly established franchise films/sequels. The highest-grossing non-franchise film is currently "F1: The Movie" in 9th place with $634 million. For the question to resolve NO, an original, non-franchise film would have to release in the next few months and gross roughly $2.3 billion worldwide to overtake "Ne Zha 2", which is virtually impossible given historical box office performances of original films. Furthermore, "Ne Zha 2" is an explicit sequel (part of a series) and the top backup options are unambiguous sequels/franchise entries. Thus, a YES resolution is near-certain.
